Electrical CoC Inspection Checklist for South African Electricians

Professional South African Electrical CoC Inspection Checklist • SANS 10142-1 Aligned • Ready for Field Use

As a South African electrician issuing a Certificate of Compliance (CoC), your inspection should be based on the latest applicable SANS 10142-1 requirements and the Electrical Installation Regulations. This checklist is intended as a practical field guide to help identify visible defects, confirm protection and earthing, and record the required test results before completing the CoC.

Important: This checklist does not replace the latest SANS 10142-1, the Electrical Installation Regulations, or your responsibility as the Registered Person.

1. General Inspection

  • Supply authority seal intact, where applicable
  • Installation appears safe and free from visible defects
  • No exposed live conductors
  • Correct voltage supply
  • Main switch accessible and correctly rated
  • Installation adequately protected against mechanical damage
  • Warning labels present where required
  • No signs of overheating or burning

2. Distribution Board (DB)

  • DB cover fitted and secure
  • All openings sealed
  • Main switch identified
  • Circuit breakers correctly rated
  • Circuit breakers securely mounted
  • Neutral bar correctly installed
  • Earth bar correctly installed
  • No double lugging unless manufacturer permits
  • Circuits clearly labelled
  • Legend schedule completed
  • Surge protection installed where required
  • Earth leakage protection installed
  • Earth leakage test button functional

3. Earthing and Bonding

  • Main earth conductor present
  • Earth electrode connection secure
  • Earth continuity conductor correctly sized
  • Water pipes bonded
  • Metal waste pipes bonded where required
  • Structural steel bonded where applicable
  • Geyser bonded
  • All exposed conductive parts earthed
  • Earth continuity verified throughout installation

4. Socket Outlets

  • Socket outlets securely mounted
  • Correct polarity
  • Earth present and connected
  • No cracked or damaged sockets
  • Socket height compliant where applicable
  • Weatherproof sockets installed outdoors
  • Socket circuits correctly protected

5. Lighting Circuits

  • Light fittings secure
  • No exposed live parts
  • Correct switching arrangement
  • Metal fittings earthed
  • Ceiling roses and junction boxes fitted correctly
  • Outdoor lighting suitably rated

6. Geyser Installation

  • Isolator within required distance
  • Isolator correctly labelled
  • Earth continuity present
  • Bonding completed
  • Wiring correctly sized
  • Drip tray installed, where required
  • Thermostat and element cover fitted

7. Stove and Oven Circuits

  • Dedicated circuit installed
  • Correct cable size
  • Isolator accessible
  • Earth continuity verified
  • Protection device correctly rated

8. Fixed Appliances

  • Air conditioners correctly isolated
  • Pool pumps correctly isolated
  • Gate motors safely supplied
  • Borehole pumps protected
  • Appliances earthed

9. Outdoor Installations

  • Weatherproof enclosures used
  • Correct IP rating
  • Outdoor wiring protected
  • Garden lighting safe
  • Pool installations comply with regulations

10. Mandatory Test Results

These tests form part of the inspection and test report required before issuing a CoC.

Continuity

  • Earth continuity conductors tested
  • Bonding continuity tested

Insulation Resistance

  • Line to Neutral tested
  • Line to Earth tested
  • Neutral to Earth tested

Polarity

  • Polarity verified at all points

Earth Leakage

  • Trip operation verified
  • Trip time acceptable

Earth Loop Impedance

  • Measured where required
  • Fault protection verified

Voltage and Fault Current

  • Supply voltage recorded
  • Prospective short-circuit current measured or calculated
  • Earth electrode resistance recorded where applicable

Common Reasons Properties Fail a CoC

  • Unlabelled DB board
  • No earth leakage protection
  • Missing earth conductors
  • Open joints in roof spaces
  • Incorrect geyser isolators
  • Socket outlets with reverse polarity
  • Broken light fittings
  • Missing bonding on water pipes or geysers
  • Undersized conductors
  • Damaged extension wiring used as fixed wiring
  • Non-compliant solar or inverter connections

Final Documentation

  • Complete inspection report
  • Complete test report
  • Record all DB test results
  • Record earth continuity values
  • Record insulation resistance values
  • Complete CoC form
  • Attach supplementary test sheets if required
  • Sign and date as Registered Person

Digital Compliance, Done Right

Use this checklist as a practical guide before completing and issuing your Electrical Certificate of Compliance.
